properties.text: Shadows hem in your first steps. A hidden letter bearing Sergeant Rolf's seal appeared among your gear, though neither his face nor the courier are known. The forsaken town of Nouraajd waits ahead, fat with unanswered whispers.
NOURAAJD - EAST
TRIAGE NOTICE - Fevered to chapel, bitten to barricade, speaking in another voice to the ditch.
The gate bell rope is tarred black: one pull for rats, two for cultists, three when no one answers.
A bundle of children's shoes waits beside the gate, each pair tied with a strip of funeral cloth.
Market ledger: flour rationed, salt vanished, debt payable in candles, knives, or confessions.
The well bucket reeks of brine and old sermons; its rope has been cut from below.
Scribe's notice: portal sigils require clean hands, paid gold, and no cult brands.
LAST CALL - Bitter beer remains. Vodka was traded for bandages before Victor began shouting.
A soot-dark ribbon is pinned near the tavern door, small enough for a frightened child.
PLEAS TO MAYOR IRVIN - rats, missing kin, ration theft, glass prayers, no answers.
A ledger page lists households in ash instead of ink; half the names are crossed out twice.
Tallow candles gutter around a jawbone relic; every flame leans toward the chapel crypt.
A basin of rainwater and finger bones bears a prayer for those who entered the catacombs.
Prayer scrap: If the glass sings after sunset, do not answer in your own name.
An abandoned stall still displays prices for bread, grave salt, and one honest night's sleep.
Scratches around the old door form a patient spiral, the mark of Marumi Baso.
The cobbles have been scrubbed clean, but rain keeps drawing red seams from between them.
This house is sealed from outside. Something inside has written prayers backward on the door.
A yellow ribbon hangs from a nail, knotted with the same care as Victor's shaking hands.
Boards cover a nursery window; lullabies are scratched into the wood from both sides.
Chalk marks count the sick: one line for fever, two for bite, a circle for dreams of glass.
CURFEW - When the night bell rings without a hand, close shutters and forget every voice outside.
CRYPT WARNING - Beren's relic sleeps beyond the wall; thieves return older than their fathers.
Pilgrim bones kneel toward the catacombs, each skull turned away from the chapel.
A heap of split skulls marks the Pritz road; Rolf's sigil is carved into the largest.
A gnawed standard leans in the dirt, its last order still legible: Hold until dawn.
Bootprints hardened in mud turn north here, then scatter as if the squad broke formation.
FISHER WARNING - The mire keeps nets, names, and fingers. Burn anything that tugs back.
A torn net is threaded with black slime and bright feathers that twitch without wind.
A drowned lantern burns under the swamp skin, lighting no road but the one downward.
A scratched arrow points back west. Beneath it: Return the relic before the mire learns your name.
The outskirts door bears three knocks from outside and one deep dent from within.
A bundle of prayer cords waits by the hut, each knot tied around a missing neighbor's name.
A shard of stained glass hums beneath soot, warm with a ritual that has not happened yet.

properties.description: Unravel the fate of Sergeant Rolf.
Clear the OctoBogz from the cave east of Nouraajd.

properties.name: skullOfRolf
properties.description: The blackened skull of Sergeant Rolf, etched with the command sigils he used to rally Nouraajd before he fell.

properties.message: The earth quakes as ratmen pour around you—common Pritscher wretches, not the colossal brute the letter promised. Where does their behemoth hide?
Dust-choked catacombs crumble as you wrench the sacred relic free.
With the relic blazing, the OctoBogz lair groans and collapses around you.

properties.label: Letter from Rolf
properties.description: A blood-smeared plea from Sergeant Rolf describing the Pritscher hordes closing around the town.
properties.text:
Lord Commander,
The nightmarish Pritschers gnaw at our barricades without pause. Locals swear their lair festers beneath the desolate ruins of Nouraajd. Our ranks thin, our walls rot. We cannot scour them alone. Send blades before all is lost.
Sergeant Rolf

properties.name: letterToBeren
properties.label: Sealed Letter
properties.description: Mayor Irvin's sealed appeal to Father Beren, ink soaked with incense and desperation.
properties.text:
Father Beren, Nouraajd sinks beneath plague and cult. I beg your guidance before the town breaks entirely.
- Mayor Irvin

properties.description: Bring Mayor Irvin's letter to Father Beren
Recover the holy relic from the catacombs
Use the relic to cleanse the OctoBogz cave
Find Victor's daughter by following tavern and town-hall clues to the courtyard.

properties.name: holyRelic
properties.description: A sanctified shard taken from the ruined chapel, blessed to purify the OctoBogz lair once the brood has fallen.

properties.name: preciousAmulet
properties.description: A weathered family talisman carried by the old woman of Nouraajd, still warm from her prayers.
Find the stolen amulet for the old woman.

properties.text: As you weave through the village, a withered woman wrings her hands beneath a sagging awning. Desperation clings to her like smoke.
Approach the old woman and ask what burdens her.
Oh, traveler... the goblins in the nearby grove stole my family amulet. Generations prayed over it, and now it's vanished into their claws.
I'll hunt down the goblin and bring your talisman home.
I'm sorry. That path is closed to me.
Thank you. The goblins are quick and mean as knives—please be careful when you seek them.
I understand. I'll keep praying that some soul answers this plea.
You found it! Please, take this coin and my blessing.
